questions.question: During a pelvic MRI, a radiologist notes that the rectum follows a curve concave forwards, closely paralleling the sacrum. Which flexure is being described?
A 49-year-old woman undergoes surgery for a low rectal tumor. The surgeon notes three lateral bends in the rectum. Which statement about these lateral flexures is correct?
options: All are convex to the left
Upper and lower are convex to the right, middle to the left
All are convex to the right
Only the middle is convex to the right

questions.question: A patient with portal hypertension develops internal hemorrhoids. Which rectal vein is primarily responsible for draining blood into the portal venous system?
A 58-year-old man presents with rectal bleeding. On examination, the bleeding site is above the pectinate line. Which artery is the main source of blood to this area?
A 35-year-old woman presents with perianal pain and swelling. The affected area is supplied by a branch of the internal pudendal artery. Which artery is involved?
A 70-year-old man is found to have a thrombosis in the middle rectal vein. Where does this vein primarily drain?
A patient is undergoing a colectomy. The surgeon must ligate the artery that is a direct continuation of the inferior mesenteric artery and supplies the upper rectum. Which artery is this?
A 60-year-old male presents with a mass at the anorectal junction, where the rectum bends sharply to form an angle of about 80 degrees. What is the name of this anatomical curve?

A 45-year-old woman is diagnosed with rectal carcinoma. The surgeon notes that the venous drainage of the rectum provides a site for portosystemic anastomosis. Which two veins are primarily involved in this anastomosis?
options: Inferior rectal and external pudendal veins

questions.question: A 35-year-old woman with a ruptured appendix was sent to the emergency department for surgery. To cut off the blood supply to the appendix, a surgeon should ligate which of the following arteries?

A 60-year-old woman arrived at the emergency room complaining of acute abdominal pain. She was diagnosed with ischemic bowel resulting from obstruction of the superior mesenteric artery. Which of the following is most likely affected by ischemia?
